{{基礎情報 公家
| 氏名 = Otoyo-no-Mikoto
| 画像 =
| 画像サイズ =
| 画像説明 =
| 時代 = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}
| 生誕 = Unknown
| 死没 = Unknown
| 改名 =
| 別名 = Otoyo-no-Mikoto, Otoyo-no-Mikoto
| 諡号 =
| 神号 =
| 戒名 =
| 墓所 = {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|lt=Shiratorizuka Kofun|lt_ja=白鳥塚古墳}} possibly
| 官位 = {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}
| 主君 = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} possibly
| 氏族 = {{ill|Owari clan|ja|尾張氏}}
| 父母 =
| 兄弟 =
| 妻   = {{ill|Mashikitohi|ja|真敷刀婢}}
| 子   = {{ill|Takeinadane-no-Mikoto|ja|建稲種命}}, {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛}}
| 特記事項 =
}}

'''Otoyo-no-Mikoto''' (''otoyo no mikoto'') was a {{ill|gōzoku|ja|豪族}} of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and one of the '''{{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}}'''.

== Overview ==
In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" he is written as '''Otoyo-no-Mikoto'''; in the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' he is written as '''Otoyo-no-Mikoto'''<ref>"Kawachi Province, Shinbetsu-Tenson, Owari-no-Muraji," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6.</ref>.

In the "Kokuzō Hongi," he is transmitted as having been appointed {{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.

His son Takeinadane-no-Mikoto succeeded him as Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his daughter {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛}} was the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The {{ill|Hikami-Aneko Shrine|ja|氷上姉子神社}} in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} enshrines {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛}}, and there is a tradition that it is the site of Otoyo-no-Mikoto's residence.

== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}

== See also ==
*{{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}
*{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}
*{{ill|List of Figures from before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

{{デフォルトソート:おとよのみこと}}
[[Category:古代日本の人物]]
[[Category:古墳時代の人物]]
[[Category:尾張国の人物]]
[[Category:愛知県の歴史]]
[[Category:尾張氏]]
[[Category:生没年不詳]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:乎止与命]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
